title: Why Am I Losing Inches but Not Weight? (Body Recomposition Explained)
introduction: Losing inches without seeing scale changes is often a positive sign of body recomposition. You may be reducing fat while maintaining or increasing lean mass and water.

sections.heading: 📏 Why Measurements Improve While Weight Stays Flat
sections.content:
| Recomp Scenario | What Happens |
| --- | --- |
| Fat loss + muscle retention | Waist drops, scale slower |
| New training stimulus | Water/glycogen masks fat loss |
| Better posture + core strength | Shape changes first |

This is especially common when strength training begins.

sections.heading: Best Progress Metrics for Recomposition
sections.content:
Track all of these:

• Waist, hips, chest every 1-2 weeks
• Progress photos under same conditions
• 7-day weight average
• Strength progression

Related: [why am i gaining weight while working out](/blog/why-am-i-gaining-weight-while-working-out).

sections.heading: How to Keep Recomp Moving
sections.content:
Use a small-to-moderate deficit, high protein from [protein calculator](/tools/protein-calculator), and progressive strength training.

Text CTA: Track body measurements plus intake in Eati so you do not abandon a plan that is actually working.

midArticleCta: Use Eati to track measurements, workouts, and calories in one place for a clearer body recomposition picture.
conclusion: Losing inches but not weight is usually progress, not failure. Keep measuring what matters and avoid making aggressive changes based on scale-only data.
metaTitle: Why Am I Losing Inches but Not Weight? Recomp Explained
metaDescription: Learn why you can lose inches without scale changes and how to track body recomposition progress correctly.
targetKeyword: why am i losing inches but not weight

faqs.question: Is losing inches better than losing weight?
faqs.answer: Often yes for body composition, because inches suggest fat reduction and shape change even when scale weight is noisy.
